quick_facts.label: Conducting body
quick_facts.value: Institute of Banking Personnel Selection (IBPS), which conducts the Common Recruitment Process (CRP) for Probationary Officer/Management Trainee posts in participating public-sector banks.

quick_facts.label: Current recruitment cycle
quick_facts.value: CRP PO/MT-XVI is the 2026 notification cycle, for participating-bank vacancies of 2027–28.

quick_facts.label: Application window (dated context)
quick_facts.value: For CRP PO/MT-XVI, online registration and online fee payment run from 1 July 2026 through 21 July 2026. As of 19 July 2026, the window is still open; applicants should use the official portal and not rely on third-party deadlines.

quick_facts.label: Baseline qualification
quick_facts.value: The XVI notification requires a graduation degree in any discipline from a Government of India-recognised university, or an equivalent qualification recognised by the Central Government; the required result/document deadline is specified in the cycle notification.

quick_facts.label: Age rule (XVI dated context)
quick_facts.value: For CRP PO/MT-XVI, the standard age range is 20–30 years as on 1 July 2026, with upper-age relaxations for eligible categories under the notification.

quick_facts.label: Selection route
quick_facts.value: The current cycle uses an online preliminary examination, online main examination, mandatory personality test, interview, and then provisional allotment subject to merit, preferences, vacancies, and verification.

quick_facts.label: Application fee (XVI dated context)
quick_facts.value: For CRP PO/MT-XVI, the stated online fee/intimation charge is ₹175 inclusive of GST for SC/ST/PwBD candidates and ₹850 inclusive of GST for all other candidates; bank transaction charges are additional where applicable.

sections.heading: What IBPS PO (CRP-PO/MT) is for
paragraphs: IBPS PO/MT is a common recruitment process rather than a university qualification. IBPS conducts it on behalf of participating public-sector banks to identify candidates for entry officer roles titled Probationary Officer (PO) or Management Trainee (MT). A candidate applies once for the notified CRP cycle and, if successful, may be provisionally allotted to a participating bank according to the applicable merit, preferences, vacancies, and eligibility checks.

The cycle number and participating banks can change. For the current XVI cycle, IBPS describes the recruitment as being for vacancies of 2027–28, so candidates should treat the detailed notification for their specific cycle—not older coaching material or prior-year notices—as the controlling document.
bullets: This exam is a route into participating public-sector banks; it is not a guarantee of appointment merely by registering or taking the examination.
The official IBPS FAQ states that eligibility criteria must be checked in the notification for the relevant CRP examination.

sections.heading: Eligibility: who can apply
paragraphs: For CRP PO/MT-XVI, the educational requirement is a degree (graduation) in any discipline from a university recognised by the Government of India, or an equivalent qualification recognised by the Central Government. This means commerce, science, arts, management, computer applications, vocational degrees, and distance-learning degrees can be relevant pathways only where the individual award and institution meet the notification’s recognition and documentation requirements.

The XVI notification’s standard age band is 20 to 30 years as on 1 July 2026. It also sets category-specific upper-age relaxations and documentary requirements. Applicants should verify nationality, category certificate, disability accommodation, ex-serviceman, and other special-case rules directly against the current detailed notice before paying the fee.

Candidates must have the required graduation result and supporting mark-sheet/degree documentation by the notification’s stated cutoff. For XVI, the notification specifies 21 July 2026 for the final result to have been declared.
bullets: Do not assume that enrolment in a final year is enough: check the result-declaration cutoff for the cycle.
Where a university awards CGPA/OGPA, retain the university’s conversion evidence if the notification requires percentage reporting.
Use the current notification to confirm age-relaxation eligibility and the prescribed certificate format.

sections.heading: Selection and examination format
paragraphs: CRP PO/MT-XVI is scheduled as a staged process: preliminary examination in August 2026, main examination in October 2026, personality test in November 2026, interview in November/December 2026, and provisional allotment in January 2027. These dates are stated as tentative and can be revised by IBPS.

The preliminary examination tests English Language, Quantitative Aptitude, and Reasoning Ability. The main examination tests Reasoning & Computer Aptitude, Data Analysis & Interpretation, General/Economy/Banking Awareness, and English Language, with a descriptive English component. The notification specifies the authoritative question counts, marks, timings, cut-offs, and negative-marking rules for the current cycle.

Candidates shortlisted from the main examination must take the personality test to be eligible for interview. For the final score in XVI, the notification assigns relative weightage of 80:20 to the online main examination and interview; candidates must qualify both as required to be considered for provisional allotment.
bullets: Preliminary examination: qualifying screening stage for shortlisting to the main examination.
Main examination: the score-bearing written stage used with interview performance for final merit.
Personality test: mandatory for interview eligibility in the XVI process; interview remains a separate assessed stage.
Always download the call letter and examination information handout when issued, because centre, reporting, and test-day instructions are cycle-specific.

sections.heading: How to apply in the current cycle
paragraphs: Apply online through the official IBPS CRP PO/MT-XVI registration portal. The portal lists 1 July 2026 to 21 July 2026 for registration and online fee payment, and it warns that submission of an application does not itself establish eligibility; IBPS may scrutinise and reject candidature later if requirements are not met.

Before submitting, read the detailed notification and portal instructions, complete registration, upload the required image/documents in the prescribed form, provide the requested academic and category details, state bank preferences where the form requests them, pay online, and preserve the application printout and e-receipt. Keep the registered mobile number and email accessible throughout the process.

The XVI schedule provides for an edit window after registration closes, with timing to be notified on the official IBPS website. This is not a reason to submit incomplete or inaccurate information: applicants should review every field and uploaded document before final submission.
bullets: Official registration/fee deadline for XVI: 21 July 2026.
The portal currently lists 5 August 2026 as the last date to print the submitted application.
Use only the IBPS portal and official IBPS notices for application, call-letter, result, and correction-window updates.

sections.heading: Preparation approach aligned to the official process
paragraphs: Build preparation around the notified test domains rather than a generic “banking exam” syllabus: English, reasoning, quantitative/data analysis, computer aptitude, and general economy/banking awareness. For the main examination, include regular descriptive-English practice because it is a separately specified component.

Use timed online practice that reflects the sectional and overall time limits in the current information handout/notification. After each attempt, diagnose errors by topic and by question-selection decisions, then revise fundamentals before increasing mock-test volume. This is particularly important because the preliminary and main stages have different purposes and content depth.

Maintain a current-affairs and banking-awareness revision system using reliable primary sources such as RBI, Government of India, and bank/regulator publications, while treating the IBPS notification as the source of truth for the test structure and selection rules. Recheck the official website for revised dates, call letters, accessibility instructions, and any corrigendum.
bullets: First establish speed and accuracy for preliminary English, quantitative aptitude, and reasoning.
Then devote substantial study time to main-level data interpretation, reasoning/computer aptitude, banking/economy awareness, and descriptive writing.
Practise writing clear, time-bounded English responses for the descriptive component rather than preparing objective questions alone.

sections.heading: What success leads to
paragraphs: Candidates who clear the required stages may be provisionally allotted as PO/MT to a participating public-sector bank. Provisional allotment is not the same as a final appointment: it remains subject to merit position, the candidate’s bank preferences, available vacancies, document/eligibility verification, medical fitness or other bank requirements, and the participating bank’s final decision.

For XVI, the notification states that interview carries 100 marks, sets minimum interview qualifying marks, and combines the online main examination and interview in an 80:20 weightage for the final score. This makes main-exam performance and interview readiness both material to the outcome; preliminary-exam performance is used for shortlisting rather than final-score weightage.

After appointment, a PO/MT enters a bank officer career path and may later pursue professional banking qualifications such as JAIIB/CAIIB, subject to the rules of the employing bank and the certifying body. Those qualifications are separate from the IBPS recruitment process.
bullets: A high score alone does not create an appointment right; allotment and joining depend on the notification and bank verification.
Vacancy numbers and participating-bank requirements are cycle-specific and may be updated by IBPS.

Do not treat a coaching-provider salary estimate as a guaranteed outcome: pay, allowances, location, probation terms, and posting are determined by the allotted employer and applicable rules at appointment.
